strengths: Effective application of media literacy: critical evaluation of sources (WHO vs. WebMD) and prioritization of reliability
Strong organizational skills: structured interview coordination, footage editing workflow and project management
Sophisticated use of digital tools for real-time reflection and cross-referencing information
Integration of cross-disciplinary knowledge (biology, psychology, English, Finnish) to enrich content
Clear linkage of ATL skills to overall project purpose and audience engagement
weaknesses: Distribution strategy lacks engagement metrics (views, comments, shares) to demonstrate platform effectiveness
ATL skills are listed but not consistently tied to specific tasks or outcomes in the production process
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: You **effectively leveraged a variety of ATL skills**, from media literacy to organization, to drive the documentary forward. The breadth of cross-disciplinary connections showcases your ability to transfer learning across subjects. To strengthen this section, **link each ATL skill to a concrete task** (e.g., how research skills informed interview questions) and include **quantitative engagement data** that evidences the impact of your distribution choices.

strengths: Convincing connection between personal experience and learning goals, using concrete examples of student mental health needs and equestrian background
Detailed, sequenced timeline of long-term and short-term goals, including reflection on delays
Clear articulation of the documentaryâs purpose, target audience and alignment with the global context of Scientific and Technical Innovation
Defined success criteria for narrative structure, visuals, sound and editing, providing a solid evaluative framework
weaknesses: Success criteria are described qualitatively but lack measurable targets (e.g., video length, number of interviews, viewership goals)
Assessment criteria appendix is external rather than integrated; key success criteria should be summarized in the main text for clarity
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: Your planning demonstrates **strong situational awareness** and purposeful alignment of personal interests with project goals. The detailed timeline and audience analysis indicate robust logistical preparation. To enhance clarity and accountability, consider **quantifying your success criteria** and weaving the assessment standards into your narrative so that readers can easily see how you will measure your projectâs success.

strengths: Detailed reflection on learning outcomes such as empathy development, project management and problem-solving with specific examples
Clear illustration of personal growth in time management, communication and confidence
Insightful discussion of academic skill development, demonstrating deep self-awareness
weaknesses: Lacks explicit evaluation of the final product against each initial success criterion
Reflection would benefit from concrete data (survey results, viewer feedback) tied to success criteria
Missed opportunity to analyze how source reliability decisions affected the documentaryâs credibility
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: Your reflection **reveals meaningful personal and academic growth**, highlighting how the project shaped your skills and mindset. For a more rigorous self-assessment, **systematically evaluate your product against each success criterion** and support your conclusions with **specific evidence** such as feedback metrics or survey results. Additionally, consider reflecting on how your research choices influenced the trustworthiness of your final documentary.

annotations.comment: You convincingly connect personal experience with learning goals, using concrete examples of student mental health needs and your own equestrian background.
Integrate the assessment criteria appendix into the main body by summarizing key success criteria directly in the text to clarify evaluation standards.
You demonstrate effective media literacy by critically evaluating sources (WHO vs WebMD) and showing how you prioritized reliable information, illustrating strong ATL application.
Your detailed timeline with clear sequencing of long-term and short-term goals, and reflection on delays, demonstrates robust planning for achieving the product.
Your organization skill application is clear, from structuring the documentary and coordinating interviews to editing footage, reflecting strong project management.

Success criteria are described qualitatively but not quantified; consider adding measurable targets (e.g., video length, number of interviews, viewership goals) to make success criteria clear and assessable.
Using digital tools for real-time reflections and cross-referencing contradictory information highlights sophisticated use of ATL media literacy skills.
You integrate transfer skills across biology, psychology, English, and Finnish to deepen your understanding of equine therapy, showcasing cross-disciplinary application.
You effectively align your project with the global context of Scientific and Technical Innovation, reinforcing its relevance and purpose.
You clearly articulate the documentaryâs purpose and target audience, laying a strong foundation for project planning and audience engagement.
Enhance your product distribution analysis by including engagement metrics (views, comments, shares) to evidence the effectiveness of your chosen social media platforms.
Your reflection clearly outlines learning outcomes (empathy, project management, problem-solving) with specific examples, demonstrating deep personal and academic growth.
While you describe personal learning, explicitly evaluating how the final product met each initial success criterion would strengthen your reflection.
Detailed personal growth examples (time management, communication, confidence) effectively illustrate the impact of this project on your skill development.
Your self-evaluation would benefit from including concrete data (survey results, viewer feedback) directly tied to your success criteria to substantiate grading decisions.
Listing ATL skills without linking each to specific project tasks reduces clarity; tie each skill to concrete examples of how it supported your process.
Your research self-assessment notes source reliability issues; reflecting on how these choices influenced your documentaryâs credibility would deepen your evaluation.
You clearly articulate the documentaryâs purpose and target audience, laying a strong foundation for project planning and audience engagement.
You articulate clear success criteria for documentary function (narrative, visuals, sound, editing) which provides a solid basis for evaluation.
